channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Transgender troops are now being identified for removal under Pentagon orders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The military services have 30 days to figure out how they will seek out and identify transgender service members to remove them from the force — a daunting task that may end up relying on troops self-reporting or tattling on their colleagues.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No big new revelations expected in Justice Department's release of Jeffrey Epstein files
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Attorney General Pam Bondi teased the public release of government documents related to wealthy financier Jeffrey Epstein during a Fox News appearance Wednesday, declaring, “Breaking news right now, you're going to see some Epstein information released.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cardinal McElroy calls for compassion in immigration policy as he prepares to lead in Washington
item.description: Cardinal Robert McElroy, the bishop of San Diego who is preparing to take over as archbishop of Washington, D.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Bharath, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump plans tariffs on Mexico and Canada for Tuesday, while doubling existing 10% tariffs on China
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump plans to impose tariffs on Canada and Mexico starting Tuesday, in addition to doubling the 10% universal tariff charged on imports from China.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and FABIOLA SANCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Ukraine rare earth minerals deal will lead to 'sustainable' future between US, Ukraine
item.description: President Donald Trump said Thursday the rare-earth minerals agreement would benefit both the U.S. and Ukraine and would provide resources that "we need for our country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Huntington, California, city hall and mayor’s home raided in $14M public funds probe
item.description: A city hall building and the residences of several former and current city workers in Huntington Park, California, have been raided in a corruption probe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK's Starmer meets Trump at White House amid divide between US, Europe over Ukraine peace deal
item.description: United Kingdom Prime Minister Keir Starmer is meeting with President Donald Trump Thursday at the White House about ending the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate committee recommends Lori Chavez-DeRemer's confirmation as Trump's labor secretary
item.description: A Senate committee voted Thursday to advance the nomination of President Donald Trump's choice to head the Department of Labor, one of the agencies named in lawsuits over moves by Elon Musk's cost-cutting team to access federal data systems.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Giving US ownership of Ukraine's minerals would help keep peace: Hassett
item.description: Kevin Hassett said in an interview with NewsNation's Kellie Meyer that these minerals' estimated worth is $20B.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump meets with UK’s prime minister about drifting US support for Ukraine
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer visited the White House to try to convince U.S. President Donald Trump that a lasting peace in Ukraine will endure only if Kyiv and European leaders are at the table as negotiations move forward with Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's firing of military brass prompts concern but little pushback from Republicans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When the Senate unanimously confirmed Gen. CQ Brown Jr. as Air Force chief of staff in 2020, President Donald Trump hailed a “historic day for America!” on social media and said he was ”Excited to work even more closely with Gen.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration says it's cutting 90% of USAID foreign aid contracts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration said it is eliminating more than 90% of the U.S. Agency for International Development’s foreign aid contracts and $60 billion in overall U.S. assistance around the world, putting numbers on its plans to eliminate the majority of U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MARK SHERMAN and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National parks already feel the effects of layoffs, even before the busy season starts
item.description: Some 1,000 NPS employees were fired, and hiring for seasonal positions was delayed. Here’s what to know about the impacts already being felt at parks, and what it could mean for the busy season.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Gov. Lombardo, AG Ford clash on immigration policies ahead of potential 2026 matchup
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo and Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford -- who could face off in the 2026 gubernatorial election -- swapped jabs in competing statements regarding guidance about immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What Medicaid cuts could mean for Minnesota children
item.description: About one in five Minnesotans are on Medicaid. That shakes out to about one in six adults and three in 10 children.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Maine lawmaker censured for posting picture of trans student on Facebook
item.description: (NewsNation) — A state representative in Maine was censured by other members of the legislature after posting a picture of a trans teen girl on her public Facebook page. The resolution passed 75-70 in a party-line vote, the Portland Press-Herald reported. Per Maine House rules, Republican Rep.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration sets the stage for large-scale federal worker layoffs in new memo
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration says federal agencies should begin planning to eliminate employee positions. The process is known as a reduction in force. Agency leaders must submit their plans by March 13.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: USAID employees given 15 minutes to clear out offices
item.description: U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees were notified late Tuesday they would be given 15 minutes to collect their belongings from their offices as the Trump administration forges ahead with gutting the agency.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Adams asks to have charges dropped after leaked letter
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyers filed a motion to have his charges dropped after someone “leaked” a letter from now-resigned U.S. Attorney Danielle Sasson, court records show.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz officially rules out a run for U.S. Senate
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz had been weighing a Senate run since fellow Democrat Sen. Tina Smith announced earlier this month she wouldn’t run in 2026. Walz expects to decide by early summer if he’ll run for another term as governor.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Candidates for NYC mayor work to get on the ballot as Cuomo looms large
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Tuesday was the first day when New York City mayoral candidates could collect signatures to gain access to the primary ballot in June. Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo continues to be favored in the race for mayor, despite not officially declaring.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Zelensky expected to visit White House amid push for minerals deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to visit the White House on Friday as Kyiv and the Trump administration work to finalize an agreement on critical minerals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: As time change nears, Nevada considers opting out of daylight saving
item.description: Don't have a heart attack, but daylight saving time is less than two weeks away. If a Nevada lawmaker has her way, it will be the last time the state "springs ahead.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump defends Musk’s ‘genius’ email to federal workers
item.description: President Trump on Monday defended tech billionaire Elon Musk after pushback over his email demanding federal workers list five accomplishments from the past week and in doing so, raising the of risk losing their jobs. “There was a lot of genius in sending it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks Department of Education, OPM from disclosing troves of sensitive personal data to DOGE
item.description: A federal judge on Monday temporarily barred the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing troves of sensitive personal data from federal agencies. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Precious water: Nevada lawmakers have decisions ahead on conservation bills
item.description: Why aren't the rule surrounding groundwater extraction the same for everyone? That's the basic question behind a bill in the Nevada Legislature, one of several proposed laws surrounding water issues this session.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dexter's Law: Pushing for tougher sentencing for animal cruelty
item.description: One Tampa Bay area lawmaker is pushing to toughen sentences for animal cruelty, specifically in cases like the shocking discovery of Dexter, a decapitated dog found at Fort DeSoto Beach.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DeSantis reappoints Moms for Liberty cofounder to Florida ethics commission
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is attempting to put Moms for Liberty cofounder Tina Descovich on the Florida Commission on Ethics for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) threw cold water on the idea of using savings from the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) government-slashing activities to send checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5
